matches.title: SSO setup loops at the IdP login page
matches.symptom: user redirected to IdP (Okta/Azure AD/Google Workspace), signs in successfully, redirected back to our login screen — loop repeats forever
matches.resolution: Almost always a SAML clock-skew issue (>5 min drift between our IdP-config NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter timestamps and the customer's IdP clock). Have customer's ops team sync the NTP servers, OR widen the assertion validity window in the SAML config (we accept ±10min by default; can push to ±15min for chronic offenders). Secondary cause: incorrect SP entity ID. Verify the entity ID in our admin console exactly matches what the customer's IdP is sending (case-sensitive).

matches.title: Customer charged twice for the same invoice
matches.symptom: duplicate charge on credit card statement, same amount, same date
matches.resolution: Confirm with Stripe dashboard (search by last-4 + amount + date range). Refund the duplicate via Stripe direct (NOT through our billing UI — it 422s on charges older than 24h). Email customer with both reference numbers + the refund ETA (5-7 business days). Route to billing_ops queue if the duplicate predates the 30-day refund window — finance has to issue a manual ACH credit instead.

input.query: export hangs 95 percent CSV large dataset

matches.title: Export job hangs at 95% for large datasets
matches.symptom: CSV/JSON export of >100k rows never completes, progress bar stuck at 95% indefinitely, job eventually shows 'failed' after 30min timeout
matches.resolution: Known issue with the streaming export's chunk-flush threshold — the last 5% chunk exceeds our 10MB write buffer for wide schemas (>40 columns). Workaround: have customer filter to <50k rows per export, OR switch to the paginated API (it streams in 10k-row chunks server-side). Fix is on the v2.4 roadmap (linked ticket: ENG-4521). Bump priority if customer is enterprise-tier — they have a contractual export SLA.

input.query: webhook HMAC signature mismatch events not arriving

matches.title: Webhook receiver returns 200 but events never arrive
matches.symptom: customer's webhook endpoint responds 200 OK in their access logs, but events never appear in their downstream system
matches.resolution: Check the webhook signature header (X-MDK-Signature). If customer's HMAC secret rotated and they didn't update both sides simultaneously, our retry queue will silently drop the event after 5 consecutive signature-mismatch failures (anti-replay defense). Have them rotate the secret, update both sides in the same change window, then trigger a webhook replay from the admin panel (Settings → Webhooks → Replay last 24h). If signature is fine, check their endpoint's processing — they may return 200 BEFORE actually persisting the event, then crash silently in their queue worker.

input.query: totally unrelated query about medieval history

matches.title: Locked out after password reset email
matches.symptom: user clicks reset link from email, gets 'link expired' error
matches.resolution: Reset links are valid for 24h from issue, single-use. Generate a fresh one from the admin console (Users → search → Send reset link). If failure repeats, check the customer's email security gateway — Mimecast, Proofpoint, and Microsoft Defender for Office 365 all pre-click links to scan them, which burns the single-use token before the actual user can click. Workaround: have the customer add our reset-email sender domain to their gateway's allowlist, OR issue them a temporary password via admin console (force-rotate at next login).

matches.title: SOC 2 Type II report request
matches.symptom: prospect's procurement team asking for SOC 2 Type II report before signing
matches.resolution: We have a current SOC 2 Type II (audited Q4 last year, covers 12 months of operations). Distribution requires a signed mutual NDA — send the prospect our standard NDA template (link in confluence: legal/templates/mnda.docx). Once signed by both sides, route to security@movate.io with the NDA attached; security ops will send the SOC 2 within 1 business day. Common follow-ups: penetration test summary (annual; same NDA covers it), data processing addendum (DPA) for GDPR/CCPA — pre-signed PDF lives at legal/dpa-v3.pdf.

matches.title: Bulk import users from CSV
matches.symptom: customer wants to onboard 100+ users at once, doesn't want to invite one-at-a-time
matches.resolution: Use the Bulk Import wizard (Admin → Users → Bulk import). Required CSV columns: email, first_name, last_name, role. Optional: team, manager_email, invite_immediately (bool, default true). Max 5000 rows per upload. Common gotcha: the 'role' column must match EXACTLY one of our defined role slugs (case-sensitive: 'admin', 'member', 'viewer' — not 'Admin'). Validate the CSV with the dry-run toggle before committing — it'll surface row-level errors without creating any accounts.

matches.title: Initial data import — what's the recommended path?
matches.symptom: newly-signed customer asking how to get their existing data in
matches.resolution: Three paths, depending on data size: (1) <10k records: use the Bulk Import wizard (CSV upload, see KB-007); (2) 10k-1M records: use the migration API (bulk-create endpoint, supports up to 100k records per request, async with progress polling); (3) >1M records: schedule a managed migration with our solutions engineering team — they have tooling for direct DB-to-DB copies from common sources (Salesforce, Hubspot, Postgres). All paths support a dry-run mode that surfaces validation errors before committing. Always recommend customers start with a sandbox tenant for their first 1-2 imports to learn the data shape.
